Please don’t remove bookmarks of company list :

  1. Don’t make other bookmarks of the same company name in the company table list.
  2. Just update questions in the respective companies bookmark.
  3. If a new company's name occurs to you, add it in the company list and questions.
  4. Also put the company name in the outline list (in side panel)

Steps to follow:

  1. Select the company name
  2. Right click on it
  3. In menu list - Add to document outline

When the shortlists are released for interviews? At the enKid nof Nov.  or earlier??+++(I don’t know any seniors, ppl who know seniors plz ask them abt this) In IIT BHUsome shortlists are being released. In IITR, it’s released in the last week of NOV . In IITK some shortlists are released. IITB, a few weeks ahead. In IITKGP last week of NOV PLEASE CONTRIBUTtring: leetcodeE.

People from new IITs, NITs, IIITs, BITS are also requested to add their test questions. indeed. Currently most of the contribution is from old IITs only. let me correct you only few Old IITs  contributing. Yes, exactly!!Everyone is contributing now.ChillYes,but still no contribution from new IIT’s,NIT’s, BITS.++

Guidelines:

Mention your college name

  • Mention CPI Cutoff and eligible discipline (please apply red color highlight)

  • While typing, use Ctrl + Enter to go to a new page
  • PLEASE KEEP COMPANIES ON SEPARATE PAGES or use horizontal lines to separate them.
  • Enable outline for Company Shortcut razorpay
  • Page edit history will be maintained in History Page
  • Please don’t remove questions, some people are frequently deleting screenshots/questions.
  • If possible, mention whether the company is open for M.Tech. or  not  ns
  • Red
  • For CPI and Discipline Cutoffs
  • Orang
  • For Very Important details
  • Pink
  • For Important details
  • Cyan
  • For a QuestionWhile adding external solution links, please apply green color highlights
  • Green
  • For External Links and Answers to any question
  • Mention CPI Cutoff and eligible discipline, please apply red color highlights
  • Please don’t edit or remove heading 1 or heading 2 style. It screws the outline and makes the document look cheap.
  • Please update here about every company help other

Amazon

Important Points

  • Discipline:                 
  • Platform:        Amcat        
  • Duration:                 
  • Question type:         

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

TAKING SCREENSHOTS NOT ALLOWED

NITS

Platform : AMCAT

We have to take 4 elements at a time and , every time we take 4 elements . we have to find the max value we can get by picking all 4*n elements.in an array of size 4*n, our values increase by the value of the 3 rd smallest element taken.

Code 1: subtree with max average value

Code: 2 :  least number of unique integers after removal (d k id O(nlogn) solution pass? or were we expected to get it in O(n)

Rest part have : Debugging ques, reasoning question etc

See this to know the exam pattern:

https://leetcode.com/discuss/interview-question/1358837/Amazon-OA-SDE1

More Question from NITs (Amazon)

amazon

IITD

Platform : AMCAT

2 Coding questions. Different for everyone

Eligibility?? no cgpa cut

Mine were -

  1. https://leetcode.com/problems/maximum-units-on-a-truck/  all test cases were not passing in the knapsack approach.
  2. https://leetcode.com/discuss/interview-question/699973/Goldman-Sachs-or-OA-or-Turnstile

Rest part have : Debugging ques, reasoning question etc

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

IITK

Platform: AMCAT

7 easy debugging/error correction at the start in 20 minutes

2 coding questions in 70 mins

                1. Amazon | OA 2019 | Substrings with exactly K distinct chars - LeetCode Discuss

                2. Amazon | online-assessment | FetchItemsToDisplay - LeetCode Discuss(ye link doesn’t exist bol raha hai)

Workstyles Amazon Test (Personality test)

Aptitude Test (24 Q / 35 Mins)

Last Compulsory Feedback form

`

Coding ques -

  1. Fill the truck : https://leetcode.com/problems/maximum-units-on-a-truck/ 
  2. Exactly as https://leetcode.com/problems/sell-diminishing-valued-colored-balls/ but in a different phrasing

Rest all sections same as above.

IITR

Platform: AMCAT

Coding questions - 70 min

  1. Exactly same as https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/count-number-of-substrings-with-exactly-k-distinct-characters/ 
  2. Given an array and a K. To find minima of all contiguous subarray of length  k and find then return max of all the minima’s.(very easy and all test passed with brute force);

        ex: arr = {2,8,5,9} K = 3;

minimum of {2,8,5}= 2, min of {8,5,9}=5;

ans = max(2,5)= 5;

Students have a different set of codYou are given a tree consisting of N nodes, numbered from 0 to N-1. Each Nodes contain one of the ing questions which were common to questions discussed above.

Rest sections were the same as mentioned above.

        

DE Shaw     

Important Points

  • Discipline:                 
  • Platform:        Hackerrank        
  • Duration: 25+35+35 minutes
  • Question type:
  • IIT KGP
  1. Given 3 arrays, count the subsets such that their sum is divisible by 7. solution? (solution: https://ideone.com/edGjJh) What does lets mean? Please clarify the question

  1. You are given an array of N integers. Given that you have to pick a non-empty subsequence, what is the probability that you pick a subsequence which has the same value of bitwise and, or and xor?

Solution:

All the numbers in the chosen subsequence should be the same.

Why?

Say, if a= 1100, b=0110  chosen then xor= 1010 or=1110 and=0100, different.

say, if 1110,1110,1110 chosen, xor=1110,or=1110,and=1110

if  1110,1110,1110,1110 chosen, xor=0000,or=1110,and=1110

if, 0000,0000,0000 chosen, xor=0000,or=0000,and=0000

if, 0000,0000,0000,0000 chosen, xor=0000,or=0000,and=0000

Thus, if number chosen are zero then length of subsequence can be odd as well as even,

but if it is non-zero then length must be odd.

So, count the frequency of all elements.

say, cnt0,cnt1,cnt2,cnt3...

res=  (2^(cnt0-1) + 2^(cnt0-1) -1) + 2^(cnt1-1) +2^(cnt2-1)+.......

PS: nc1+nc3+nc5+nc7....= 2^(n-1)

    nc0+nc2+nc4+nc6+...= 2^(n-1)

  1. Given a cost for each of the digits from 0 to 9, what is w largest string you can create using exactly K amount?merge s

 2021):

please share solutions

For Placements(Test date:03/10/2021) IITR :- 

Could anyone solve questions 3,5,6? Please give the soln.

Question 3 Soln:

Numbers distributed in groups and merged in such a way that makes a lexicographically largest array. That means basically the array is sorted in order. Final problem is to find minimum number of swaps to make array into sorted array.

$ This kind of problem can be easily solved using graphs.

$ Make a graph with N nodes and join node i to node j if ith node should be present on jth index

$ This will create several non-intersecting cycles, in which a cycle of 2 nodes will require 1 swap and cycle of 3 nodes will require 2 swaps.

$ Therefore a cycle with n nodes will require n-1 swaps.

$ Find cycles (connected components problem)

$ Find number of nodes in each cycle (traverse in each cycle), say n, then no of swaps in that cycle = n-1

$ find Total swaps

        Intuit( 2 tests, at IIT Kgp )media

Important Points

  • Discipline: Plz add discipline  FTE or intern?        
  • Duration:                 
  • Platform:        Glider(test 1) + Hackerearth(test 2)
  • Question type
  • Test 1
  • Time - 90 minutes
  • Questions can be  done in any order and can be visited later.
  • Proctoring was done 121 with a chrome extension that records the screen, so if you open anything else you can get into trouble. Camera and mic were turned on.Also strict posture rules, you can’t see outside screen lol !!! WTFF, What if you want to write the example corner cases and check lol

        

  1. There are N rides in an adventure fair. Person can go for the first ride at most N times, second ride at most N-1 times and so on. There's an array of tokens, where token[I] is the number of tokens for i-th ride. You have a total of k tokens. Maximize the number of rides (ining repeated clouds)
  2. Given a string, the master string is obtained by repeating the string an infinite number of times. You are given q queries,                 each query having L , R and a character C. Find the number of instances of C in the master string in [L, R]
  3. Mirror a binary tree
  4. Given an array of passwords, each password is tried out in increasing order of its length, until the correct password is found. If more than 1 password has the same length, then random order among them. Each password takes 1 second to be tried out. After every k failed attempts there's a penalty of 5 seconds. Find the min and max time required to find the correct password (given).(its on codeforces)                                                                                                                    

  • Test 2
  • Test 2(1 hr)- only for peeps who solved all 4 problems in Test 1, IIT kgp
  1. Prefix Sums based problem on STRING
  2. Greedy problem on array based on idea of Median, min moves to put all elements  together. ( tricky and hard implementation)

IIT Dhanbad and IIT KGP Internship or placement ??

1.Given an array of size n and q queries. Each query contains an integer d.

For each query we need to print whether it is possible to select at most two distinct integers from the array such that the three selected no. forms a valid triangle or not. n<=1e5, q<=2*1e6, d<=1e6

2.Given an array of size n, find  the subsequence is a multiple of m(could be 0 as well) ie abs(A[i]-A[j])=k*m for any two indexes in subseq.

3. Given two strings A and B. Find length of longest palindrome that can be formed using characters from two palindromic substring A and  |A|, |B|<=1e5

Important Points: FTE or 6month?

  • Discipline:         CSE ECE EEE MAT        -- IIT(BHU)
  • Platform: Hackerrank
  • Duration: 1 hour
  • Question type: 2 coding from medium to tough and 20 MCQ based on dsa, debugging and logical ability.

  1. You have a broken calculator, only the given digits are working, the only operation you can use is multiply and equal. You are given a number, find the minimum number of clicks on the calculator to obtain the given number. ```````````````  Test case <=100 N<=106 . Example: n= 60, working
  2. digits are 2,3,4,5 Possible      solutions 3*5*2*2=60, 3*5*4=60 Answer is 6(clicks counted till equal to)
  3. Running 1: lk

how to do that broken calculator? r u sure it is 10power(6) or just 10power(4)?

Tip: All debugging mcqs were in java, study java stacks and list. Also see the garbage collector on java. That will be sufficient.

1 Rippling

 Important Points

  • Discipline: CS, MnC, EE ...was Mtech CSE allowed?????No. CLG?
  • CPI Cut-off: 0.00 (No CPI cut-off)
  • Platform: Hackerrank
  • Duration: 3 ques in 1.5 hr
  • Question type: 3 ques,one from painter-partition(medium),another from segment tree (medium-hard)and another super easy

First ques was Painter-Partition Problem The painter's partition problem

 Second ques was based on Segment Trees but was passing through brute forces approach too
https://discuss.codingblocks.com/t/proble-asked-in-sabre-campus-hiring-test/108125/7

Third ques was “There are N people standing in queue each of them are either facing left or right .these people can listen to a person if they are facing towards that person, so we need to find a leader among N peo inple to whom most of the people can listen,Our task is to find maximum possible number of people who can listen to their leader.”

Can be done by making two array(namely left and right) of size N,one(namely right) should be filed by traversing from forwards direction and increasing the count whenever we encounter a person in right direction,same applies to another(namely left) array which is traversed from backward and increasing the count whenever we encounter a person standing in left direction. and then finding maximum in left[i]+right[i].

Q1 - https://drive.google.com/file/d/1-Piwr4gM-iC20Q9W5NaT73mFyZ-y3keS/view?usp=sharing  [40 pts]

Q2 - https://drive.google.com/file/d/1xDbjGMVCKkJnQ0Ct8-wfaYEdcYsGhiym/view?usp=sharing [80 pts]

Q3 - https://drive.google.com/file/d/1b4KUE1S1MojrVHubGOc6H_MECfJEHfVE/view?usp=sharing [30 pts]

IITG

  • Job Profile: Software Engineer

  • Eligibility :
  • Bachelors: Only B.Tech CSE, MNC, ECE, EEE
  • CPI Cut-off: 6.80 (Company changed cpi cutoff twice, first it was 0 ie no cpi cutoff then changed it to 6.5 and finally to 6.8)
  • Selection Process:
  • 1. Resume Shortlisting
  • 2. Online Technical Test (60 mins Coding Round)
  • 3. Video conferencing (Skype Interview)
  • Coding Round: Hackerearth, 60 mins, 3 Coding Ques: Easy, Medium, Hard

 

0

IIIT Hyderabad (28 Nov)

        3 ques , 1hr

  1. N containers, each having some litres of water in it. Two arrays(of length N) given:

W : Wi = water (in litres) that is currently in tanker i

C: Ci = Max capacity (in litres) of tanker i

Water from one tanker can be transferred to another tanker.

Determine the min no of tankers that can transport the same volume of water as currently being transported by N tankers.

Sample input

3 -> N

2 2 2 -> W

4 4 4 -> C

Sample output

2

(Sum total of W = 6L. 6L can be transported using 2 tankers of max capacity = 4L)

  1. N*M matrix given initialized with values between 1 and 20. From every cell (i,j), you are only allowed to jump either on cell (i+mat[i][j], j) or (i, mat[i][j]+j), with the condition that the jump should be within the matrix (valid). Determine the min no of steps in which one can reach from (1,1) (first cell) to (N,M) (last cell). Matrix is 1 indexed.

Constraints:

1<=N<=1000

1<=M<=1000

Important Points: Is the test proctored?  No ,same questions for all appearing.

     3 questions are there or 4?? here 4 are given, that’s why asking

  1. Discipline:         IS IT OPEN FOR ALL branches?? YES(IIT Dhanbad )
  2. Duration: 1 hr 30 mins
  3. Question Type: 15 mcqs and 3 coding questions.

(MCQs were aptitude or CS topics??)

  • https://www.hackerearth.com/practice/basvic-programming/implementation/basics-of-implementation/practice-problems/algorithm/milly-and-chocolates-ii-65198198/
  • https://ideone.com/sYxln3 What is the exact question here?++10000000 Stated following is the question
  •  There are Q queries and a binary tree with N nodes such that level-order traversal numbering will read 1,2,3,...N
  • Query type 1: Request Query: need to push the color of the Vth node in the result,c,2*v,n);
  •                 color(tree,c,2*v+1,n);t vector
  • Query type 2: Update Query: need to update the subtree rooted at V to have Cth(Update Query number) color. Code??
  • Given n numbers of students in a classroom each having a couple of pencils, you are to find out how many pencil mountains you can make by collecting all the pencils in the classroom.
  • Assume One pencil is a singular unit and cannot be broken into multiple pieces.
  • (n<=30)  (eg. n=3, output=5). What is a pencil mountain?(Same as that of a valid bracket sequence, replacing each ‘(‘ with a / and ‘)’ with a \ and after that stacking each consecutive /’s like a big slash line, same for backslash) haan to catalan number hi hua na???

In which college were these 3 questions asked?? IIT Dhanbad

diff questions for diff people?? NO

        IITG Questions?? PLEASE ADD+++++

IITG

  1. Find two numbers from their sum and XOR.
  2. Given  two arrays of integer select values from A and B of same size.Array A values can be 1 or 2. Array B can take any value. Also given an integer m. We have tom array B such that the sum of selected values must be greater than or equal to m. Let t denote the corresponding values of the array selected in A. we have to minimize the value of t. (Question language was different but we have to do this only)

https://codeforces.com/problemset/problem/1475/D

example:

n = 5,m = 7

A: 2 1 1 2 1

B:5 3 2 1 4

Ans : 2

We can select at index 1 and 4 resulting in 7 which is >= m. Therefore the corresponding sum of values of array A is 1+1 = 2.

  1. https://leetcode.com/discuss/interview-question/906248/Interview-Question/768089

Important Points

Discipline:         

  • Platform:        HackerRank

Please add the questions here??+inf

Interview Experience:

Round 1:  Heavily based on OOPS and OS (With proper syntax.).

                        What is Object Pool (Similar to the thread pool). Design an interface for it. (It was very subjective, there’s not one correct answer for this. They                         just wanted to see the approach).

                        2 basic coding questions. First, Depth of a tree. Second, Check if 2 strings are anagrams.

Round 2: Two coding questions, two puzzles.

  1. Coding Q1. Largest square in a binary n x n matrix (s.t. all 4 corners are 1)
  2. Puzzle Q1: A farmer has 3000 bananas and 1 camel and the camel at a time can carry at most 1000 bananas at a time. The Farmer has to take these 3000 bananas 1000 km away. The problem is that the camel eats 1 banana per km and doesn't move without eating bananas. What is the maximum number of bananas he can take to the destination?
  3. Coding Q2: https://www.interviewbit.com/problems/word-ladder-i/
  4. Puzzle Q2: There's a boy whose school gets overs at 3 PM and his father picks him up at exactly 3 PM daily. They reach home daily at the same time. One day school gets overs at 2 PM and the boy starts walking towards home and father picks him somewhere in the middle and they reached home 18 minutes earlier than usual time. How much time did the boy walked?

IITKGP:

Q-1) SOL - https://www.careercup.com/question?id=5647083983863808

       

Q-2)

SOL -

long biohazard(int n,vector<int> allergic, vector<int> poisonous){

    int dp[n+1];

    vector<int> adj[n+1];

    for(int i=0;i<allergic.size();i++){

        adj[poisonous[i]].push_back(allergic[i]);

        adj[allergic[i]].push_back(poisonous[i]);

    }

    dp[0] =0;

    int max_high =0;

    for(int i=1;i<=n;i++){

        dp[i] = dp[i-1];

        int high =0;

        for(intj=0;j<adj[i].size();j++){

            if(adj[i][j]<i){

                high = max(high ,adj[i][j]);

            }

        }

        max_high = max(max_high.high);

        dp[i]+= (i-max_high);

    }

    return dp[n];

}

Coding: 

Array Streak:

Ray has bought alexa and is testing the functionality of it. While testing, he came up with an idea to ask alexa N random integers and find the streak array for the same. He has randomly chosen a number K and want to find the streak the elements are increasing by atleast k then the streak will keep on increasing else the streak starts with 1 again.Ray is working fine with the small N values but as the N gets large, he is unable to keep the count. He needs help to maintain the streak aray. Can you help him?

Input Format

The first line of input consists of two space-separated integers N and K.          The second line of input consists of N space-separated integers denoting the array elements.

Output Format:

Print the array which represents the streak array holding above condition

-> make graphs (graph_w(i)) corresponding to every unique weight.

-> find max nodes and corresponding max product for each graph.

IIT DELHI

One question in one hour. Simple DP. (Can also be done with greed)  - DOES EVERYBODY HAD SAME QUESTIONS ? YES

       College: IITM

Platform: Hackerrank

Duration: 50 min

Questions: 1 Coding Question

Question: (EXACT SAME QUESTION WAS ASKED IN CITRIX OA) [Please Don’t delete questions!] Yes please

Minimum Sideway Jumps 

You are given an array of size n, and num k, and a team size s, you are to pick s numbers from the array following certain constraints and return their sum,

  1. To pick a number from the array, you have to consider the first k  and last k elements from the array, and pick the largest one among them, remove it from the array and continue the process until you pick the team size elements, if you have the same number from first k and last k, pick the one with least index. Finally return the sum of s elements picked.

arr = {18, 5, 11, 9, 7, 12} , s=3, k=2

        5, 11, 9, 7, 12 -> 18

        5, 11, 9, 7 -> 12

        5, 9, 7 -> 11

        return 18+12+11 = 41.        Which institute? IITD: Then what about the question just above this, because someone mentioned there everybody had the same question?(someone clarify)?+1+1

        same code-        https://ideone.com/4uX0kB 

c++ code https://ideone.com/kz9j7x

https://ideone.com/tr9KV1

priority_queue + 2 pointer approach -> https://ideone.com/1pX0C 

 Accepted code ?? how to do it in an optimized way ?? (could someone upload solution using priority queue)

How I’m approaching (Not solved though): I am thinking of creating a list of tuples (element, index). Create a max heap. In it, push k first and k last elements once. Now, a loop of s operations, in which store and delete the head element. Now either we will push the right element(if it's from first k elements) of the deleted element in the heap, or left element (if it's from the last k elements) in the deleted element in heap. But , I’m stuck on how to check which one we will push. Because the index of other elements doesn’t change after deletions. Can someone tell how to go further? :  Instead of storing indexes in tuples, just store if the element is from front or back.

Can we solve this question in O(N*S) Time Complexity? [S can be till N so better to use NlogN approach] store the element in the priority queue with their index. and tak two pointer left and right if the top element is from left then left++ else right --;

2 test dene ke baad hua yeh ……...

EDGE FOCUS:

Which institute? IITD

Each section had a timer.

There were :

1.probability Qs(Expectation and poisson distribution)(medium-hard level)

(MCQs and Subjective(only final numerical value needs to be written in the blank) )

2.Theory MCQs(one section was single correct answer type and the other had multiple-correct answers) on ML(bias-variance tradeoff, Decision tree learning,cross validation)

(Concept lvl-> easy-medium(Not very deep but you need to know a wide range of concepts to answer multi-correct ones).

There was a Coding section where we are supposed to build the model there itself(common syntax for reference was provided but still need to have recent practice to solve them- 2 Qs(60 mins ig) ) [Build the model using Python APIs right? Or we had to write algorithm from scratch? ] - Yeah, we were given libraries, we just had to analyze features/variables

At last there was Devops a single Devops question-something to do with ubuntu commands(I didn’t even understand the Question,lol)

Overall medium-hard lvl.

Tip: practice basic algos like logistic regression and SVMs.

Poisson Qn : lambda for the given distribution of a random variable X=2., there’s another random variable Y=X+1, find E[Y^2] and E[1/Y].

PS:This was for the Quantitative researcher role, I heard that even SDE profile had no DSA coding questions(not sure), SDE guys plz upload Qs/topics covered here.

Platform : Hackerearth

Some Debugging Questions

2 Programming Questions

IIT BHU:   (open to all , CPI>=6.5)

time:1hr 15min

7 mcq’s(1 aptitude,6 based on output type questions(tough),among them 2 are on pointers)

2 programming questions(logic less,nearly brute force type)(chutiya question a++++++++++++++)

q1)given an array on N numbers,and need to give output how many consecutive sequences are there,these sequences length should be more than 1.and these output sequences should be in the order as they appeared in the array

example:

Input:[1,3,4,5,10,11,1,18,19,5,11,12,13,18,19,3,4,5]

output:

SEQ:3,4,5 OCCURRENCES:2

SEQ:10,11 OCCURRENCES:1

SEQ:11,12,13 OCCURRENCES:1

SEQ:18,19 OCCURRENCES:2

The output should be exactly as shown above

Q2)similarly as above ques with slight modification in output,this time with the same difference(in above ques this difference in +1) between two consecutive elements.  Anyone with an approach??

ex:

Same questions in IIT Madras

Chargebee: 7 MCQs and 2 coding Qs,

1.MCQs were on C pointers , find the error in given code , one aptitude Qn(lvl medium-hard)

2. Coding section was shit ,2 long ass questions with no thinking/logic (Even the constraints were not given. If we think of some approach couldn’t even have an idea if this would give a TLE)

You might need to use data structures like map< vector<int> ,int> or Equivalent DS(for Q1).

the questions were about

(1) finding sequences in a given array of numbers

(2) find all groups of sequences with duplicates and sub-sequences and print them ordered by the size of the sequence.

PS: Total time 75 mins, 6 MCqs- less than 10 points(Total)(Each MCQ would take at least 3-4mins),

      Coding section- first Q-30 points(long ass) ,secondQ-50 pointf

Tips: Don’t waste time on MCQs, try to create classes for the 50 point question(Apparently they suggested this, I didn’t notice).

Q1) In an array need to find 2 numbers whose diff is nearest to a given number. If more than one such pair exists, return the pair whose sum is minimum   Approach -

Q3 -> graph + bfs with last node as source. Isn’t this question asking for the shortest path in a directed graph ?  Yes Exactly https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/shortest-path-for-directed-acyclic-graphs/ I think this is the solution with all the weights equal to 1

Q2 -> min subarray with sum at least K is O(n2) sol accepted?

https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/minimum-length-subarray-sum-greater-given-valueSmallest subarray with sum greater than a given value/ 

worldwas there partial marking? What can be the cutoff(solving all 3?)         

Are these questions same for both the profiles MTS and MTS for Machine Learning Team? Yes Two pointers Is O(NlogN) complexity accepted?

Q2) A knight needs to fight with a queue of people with energy levels H_i and he fights with two people simultaneously. If the length of the array is greater than 2, he spends an energy equal to the max of two elements he fights and if he fights with a single person then corresponding energy is spent. While selecting he selects out of the first two three people in the queue. Find the best way to fight with people such that energy is spent min and worst way such that energy is spent max. Same as du IIT Dhanbad Q.1 If anyone found solution to this problem please share

Q3) In an infinite grid there is a house which is connected to adjacent houses which can be reached by cost 1 and there are N secret connections between houses which cost k.  Given a source and destination find min cost to reach destination. Please make the question clearer or add screenshot++

Please add solutions/approaches, if anyone has.

                IIT BHU:

  • //Idea is that find the next permutation for "k" times and then after that swap the
  •         //characters untill the original string and permutation
  •         //become equal and everytime u swap increment the count and once the strings become
  •         //equal return count.
  •         '''
  •         
  •     string res=num;
  •     for(int i=0;i<k;i++) next_permutation(num.begin(),num.end());
  •     int swap_count=0;
  •     for(int i=0;i<res.length();i++){
  •         if(res[i]!=num[i]){
  •             int start=i+1;   // search from i+1 index
  •             while(res[i]!=num[start]) start++;    //find the index to swap
  •             while(i!=start){
  •                 swap(num[start],num[start-1]);    // swap untill the characters are at same index
  •                 start--;
  •                 swap_count++;
  •             }
  •         }
  •     }
  •     return swap_count;
  • }
  • };
  • 2.Given a grid whose bottom most left coordinate is (x1,y1) and topmost right coordinate as (x2,y2). you are given a circle on this 2d plane of radius “r” and centre (xc,yc). The centre may or may not be inside the grid. Find the total number of points of the grid that lie inside the circle. O(n) was expected). 50 marks
  • 3. Given an undirected graph, you are given some nodes who are named master. Initially each node can reach only one special node or none(each connected component has at max one special node). Find the maximum no. of connections that can be made without violating the property(each connected node should have only one special) and the minimum cost of making these connections. Here the cost of making a connection is zero if the connection is between two nodes of the same connected component, else it is equal to the product of the size of the two connected components(obviously after the connection they will become connected). 80 marks

 IITM(Re-test due to Technical issues in the first test):

  1. Largest Prime Number possible from a subsequence of a Binary String.
  2. 2.Given n strings (only with lowercase alphabet),there are q queries of the form “l r k”, for each query, return the kth character in the string formed by merging strings from l to r and then sorting(in alphabetical order).
  3. 3. There are n vertices and n-1 edges(tree-like structure), each have a cost[i], determine the edge for which the difference in sum of costs of vertices present on  either side of the edge is the least.

explanation: If we remove the edge, the whole graph would be split into two connected components, let A be the sum of costs of vertices present in the first component and B for second.Now find the edge for which abs(A-B) is least.

IITK

Role: Quantitative Researcher

Platform: Hackerrank

CPI Based Shortlisting (8+) Open to all ?? or only circuitalOpen to all

the questions were easy . the only tough part was I didn't know python.

2 hours, 4 Questions.

1.Stock Maximize (to be done only in Python)

        2.Global_Maximum.cpp:

Question & Solution GFG (https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/maximum-of-minimum-difference-of-all-pairs-from-subsequences-of-given-size/)

3. Multi-label image classification prediction:

Processed images were represented as a vector of 300 attributes, with 294 values between (0,1)  and other values as 0/1 value for each feature. (294 features are input and the rest 6 are output, i.e the values to be predicted)

Testing and training csv files (with each row as vector as described above) were given, we need to submit the csv file of prediction (i.e. whether a certain feature is present or not).

Accuracy metric was used to judge the problem.(what accuracies were you getting?)(one vs all?) .

only test input was given can’t check test accuracy

Was sklearn/any other ML library allowed? sklearn was allowed

Was tensorflow allowed? Nop

4.Missing Stock Prices

No aptitude test?Nope

JEE all

  1. Unique path in grid ((0,0)->(m-1,n-1)) easy dp

 UIDAI

        IIT BHU

CGPA:0.00 , Open for all branches (btech only), 10th: 75% , 12th: 75%

Uidai has 3 hour long question paper having quantitative    apti, basic data interpretation, logical reasoning, spatial reasoning, 2 debugging section and one coding section

Each section had separate fixed timing

Questions for the coding section were different for different candidates. Barring the coding section, other sections were pretty easy.

My coding questions: uidai questions.pdf

Please give Solution of 1st question in O(q) TC done: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1C2kv-tEYLVpuDXUcU0-CU2yS6zcia0hy/view?usp=sharing

IITG:

Uidai has 3 hour long question paper having quantitative apti, basic data interpretation, logical reasoning, spatial reasoning, 2 debugging section and one coding section

Each section had separate fixed timing

Questions for the coding section were different for different candidates.

One Set:

  1. Return all the prime numbers between 2 to N, N<10^9; I tried the sieve method and all test cases ran.
  2. Second question was relatively good for everyone. One of them was : Given an array initially , try to make the array palindrome by merging(adding) some consecutive elements inside the array. In this way find a palindromic array of maximum size.

Eg: arr = {15,10,15,37,25,15} -> {15,25,37,25,15}. Size of array <=10^3.

Slice

my c++ code was not working in the actual test just now (which I am sure were correct because i have done those questions before) and there were wrong options in mcqs as well. something is wrong on their end acc. to me.

yes many MCQs had no right options

Their platform tested everything was shit++. Threw me out of test due protocol violations even when I did nothing. C++ code (even cout <<"helloworld";) was not getting compiled. And finally many MCQ didn't have right ans in option

Yeah, the options in MCQs were wrong.

In coding questions the input format was also not specified, it asked to complete the function but code-editor had no function pre-written++

Had the same problems in IITG…..

IITK

Platform: https://quicktest.calyxpod.com  (Shitty platform as mentioned by others)

Questions: 15 Aptitude (MCQ) + 2 Coding question

MCQs :- Even options in some MCQs were wrong.

Coding question:-

1. Super Egg Drop - LeetCode (60 points)

2. https://www.codechef.com/problems/FLIPCOIN/ (30 points WTF)   APPLY MATKARNA BC ISME. CHUTIYA TEST HAI. 

Experience :-

  • I was not able to run my code in mock tests as I mentioned above. I mailed the helpline as well. They told me to troubleshoot the network by setting this up :- https://developers.cloudflare.com/1.1.1.1/setting-up-1.1.1.1/windows/. Luckily I was able to run the code in the original test.
  • I was trying the second question with segment tree, but my test case was not passing. So I settled with the Bitset method.
  • Bad environment, wrong questions, no custom tests, trivial test cases
  • A google form has been rolled out (deadline to fill - 10th October 11:59p.m.) for a RETEST for students who faced issue with the test platform

IIT BHU

CGPA :7.50 , Opened for MTECH , Department(s) : cse eee ece phy mat(mnc) {resume shortlisting as well(for other departments only)}

17 questions:

15 mcqs: aptitude

2 coding question:

  1. A company hierarchy is given with one root (n-ary tree). root knows information. information is needed to be known to all the employees. Time needed to pass the information from parent to its child is 1 hr. Find minimum time so that all the employees(nodes) know the info.

test case:

IITM

CGPA: >= 8

Eligibility: Open to All

Platform: DoSelect

Time: 1.5 hrs

Format: 3 Coding questions only

  1. given string with s[i] = ‘F’ indicating “fresh” mango and s[i] = ‘R’ indicating “rotten” mango. In one day, a rotten mango causes any adjacent fresh mangoes to also become rotten. Return number of fresh mangoes remaining after k days
  2. There are N employees numbered 1 to N. Given array A of length N. Let A[i] = X (1 - indexed). If X = -1, then i-th employee has no manager. Else employee X is the manager of employee i. An employee A is “senior“ to employee B if:
  1. A is the manager of B
  2. C is manager of B and A is senior to C

                An employee can have at max one manager. Also, an employee cannot be the manager of themself.

                We need to create a grouping of employees, such that every employee belongs to one group exactly and no member in a group is senior to any other member in the same group. Return the minimum number of groups that satisfy these conditions.

  1. Given two strings S and T, with len(S) <= len(T), return minimum substring of S that contains all characters of T. If T contains duplicate characters, all occurences of the duplicate characters should be present in the substring

IIT DHANBAD:Same as IITM

IIT ISM Dhanbad

duration?

platform?

webcam?

MCQs + Three Coding Questions

        There are m places defined on the X-axis out of which n need to be travelled, starting from 0 and the defined positions can be positive as well as negative. Each unit of travel takes a unit cost. Find the min cost for travelling n places.

One question was on Banker’s algorithm.

One more question of coding was probably based on graphs. (Don’t remember exactly - If anyone remembers, please update this)

IIT D

duration?2hr

platform?doselect

webcam?yes

4 sections : 2 section for MCQ and 2 for coding

section 1: mcq aptitude :easy but less time, ~0.7 minutes/q, around 20 q

section 2: mcq aptitude :easy to medium but less time, ~0.8 minutes/q ,around 20 q

section 3: 2 coding questions (validate sudoku puzzle and find total number of dropped packet in leaky bucket given rate of outgoing packet(fixed), incoming packet array, capacity of bucket, both were easy, total time ~40 min)

section 4: 1 coding question (related to bfs graph, medium, ~35 min, but the question description was very poor and it took 10-15 minutes just to understand it!)

IMP: time for each section was fixed and you can not move from one section to another i.e you must move sequentially by submitting the current section and then moving to the next. also you cannot change/edit/modify a section once submitted.

IIT Delhi PLS ADD QUESTIONS c++

Paytm :

IITM(No CG Cutoff But only CS/EE) was it open for Mtech CSE??? YES

CS-B.tech/M.tech/Dual      EE-B.tech

Q1. Given an integer n, return the last non-zero digit in n! (factorial of n) -(3 marks)

Example:

Input : n=10

Output : 8

Explanation: 10!=3628800 , the last non-zero digit is  marked with green here.

Solution for Q1 : https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/last-non-zero-digit-factorial/

Q2.https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/subtract-two-numbers-represented-as-linked-lists/ - (3 marks)

Q3. Given a binary tree, return the sum of alternate node values of the tree - (5 marks)

If at level 1(root) the sum of values of all nodes is x1, at level 2 -x2..

return x1+x3+x5+...

Approach for Q3: while doing BFS keep the count of present level

(You can find the size of queue first and run a for loop and then increment level outside the loop so that you can know the current level every time )

PS: There were only 3 test cases per question which are also very small.(They will surely check with other hidden test cases)

IITM

Platform: Google Form (how students submitted their answers, by typing or uploading their answers/explanation in a file?) normal typing

Time? Don’t remember exactly but was more than sufficient (75 mins ig)

Eligibility: Open for all

CGPA: >=8.5

Role: ML Quantitative Researcher

Subjective Questions

  1.  Write down as many market events/data that can influence the Apple stock that other people will usually not think of? (This question expects novel and creative answers)      
  2. Which machine learning algorithm do you believe is the best to use to model and predict Apple stock's future prices using the events/data that you found above and why? Describe how you would set up and train the model. The more detailed, specific, and convincing the answer, the better.

If anyone has an answer, Please upload as soon as possible . We will have a test tomorrow at IIT KGP.

  1. 10 Aptitude Questions

Apti ques were really very easy.

  1. 3 coding questions, Everyone got different questions
  1. Give an array of N numbers, choose K largest numbers.
  2. Given an array of positive numbers as magnitude of charges and a string denoting magnitude of each of ith charge(string of characters ‘P’ and ‘N’). determine the sum of all charges and return its magnitude(Basically sum over the array along with sign and return the absolute of the sumq                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        )
  3. Given an array of N numbers, and a number K. We need to group elements of the array in such a way that each group has at least k elements in it and the difference between maximum and minimum values of any group does not exceed at given number m. Following the above two conditions, we need to return the minimum number of groups  possible. return -1 if not possible. 

Someone please add solution to 3rd problem or a Link to a similar problem (Will sorting + greedy work?) -I don’t think soIn the middle we can’t greedily decide which group this particular element belongs to),Can someone please mention the constraints? I believe we are wasting time trying to think of an O(nlogn) solution. Btw I don’t think even Binary searching on answer works - sort array (nlogn) + dp (n), for dp, let dp[i] represent minimum partitions for arr[:i]

Can you explain the dp part, How will you take care of group size being at least “k” in O(n) ?Anyone can answer. solution link plxx

IITM

-1.APTITUDE(10Qs -> easy-medium level)

Coding 3 Qs-

My set:

  1. Same as IITK-Given an array of N numbers, choose K largest numbers and return their sum

     b.  Same as IITK-Given an array of positive numbers as magnitude of charges and a string denoting magnitude of each of ith charge(string of                           characters ‘P’ and ‘N’). determine the sum of all charges and return its magnitude(Basically sum over the array along with sign and return the                                absolute of the sum)

   c. Interesting Question- Given two integers m,n. return the no.of ways of making a single connected  component in a grid of size m*n (diagonals are not considered neighbors).

Example 1:

Input              :  m=2,n=1    

Output           : 3

Explanation :Consider the numbering of cells as given below:

                        1  

                        2

Then valid combinations are : (1),(2),(1,2)

NOTE: There should be only one single connected component ,no matter the size.

Example 2:

Input              :  m=2,n=2    

Output           : 13

Explanation :Consider the numbering of cells as given below:

                           1   2

                           3   4

Then valid combinations are : (1),(2),(3),(4),(1,2),(1,3),(2,4),(3,4),(1,2,3),(1,2,4),(1,3,4),(2,3,4),(1,2,3,4)

PS: Seemed pretty easy at first but wasn’t able to solve it. Could be a hard one/I might be missing something either way please share the approach/solution, If you get it. Constraints?They were not provided  PLEASE TELL WHOEVER COULD SOLVE IT

Topics: CS basics, few output based qn’s, IP address based qns, encryption based qns etc; (Could you please add few questions which you remember)++++PLEASE+++

  1. There is a device connected to a network. IP address of the device is 192.168.1.129 and the network uses a subnet mask of 255.255.255.192, what is the correct network address for the network.(option (A) is the answer?)(NO)ans->(B)
  1. 192.168.1.192
  2. 192.168.1.128
  3. 192.168.1.129
  4. 192.168.1.1

2) Identify the correct statement describing a checksum (ans?)

3) which is the explanation of direct traversal attacks?

4) What is the hostname in the URL: https://www.example.co.jp   (isn’t the hostname www.example.co.jp)??? I think this is a subdomain name (ans?) (B) or C?

  1. co.jp
  2. example

     (C) www

     (D)https

5)  6 (O(1),O(logN), O(N), or O(NlogN)) worst time? It was only mentioned time

 ans-  O(1)??yes

6) appropriate explanation of cache

7) which features has improved in IPv6 compared to IPv4

8) Networking protocol that provides secure file transfer between different computers (SSH, SFTP, SMTP, or HTTP) ans - SFTP??yes Thanks

Webcam: No

Time: 20 min + 40 min

IITD 

30mcq ques 20 min -> exact same mcq questions as mentioned above.

one very very easy coding question of 40min that a CS student can solve in less than 10 min.

  • no logic or algo required
  • just implement the question, in python it took only 5 lines of code.
  • input was already given, just complete the function.
  • no proctoring(in the whole exam) but i will advise not to switch tabs (you never know).

IIT KGP

30mcq ques 20 min -> exact same mcq questions as mentioned above. When was the test? at 10pm

one coding question of 40min out of (shop of RPG, viz of IVY, change directory and *) that anybody can solve in less than 10 min. Though, the IDE was shitty, got hanged after every run and threw an error, the inputs got accepted only after resetting the IDE twice or thrice. So, when you know your code is correct and still throwing an error, pls try to refresh the ide and and paste you code again

  • No proctoring
  • No logic required in code

-both hostname and domain name of https://www.example.co.jp ??

No coding; All MCQs.

Aptitude: Pattern + verbal + LR + Data interpretation + probability (difficulty level?) The questions were not difficult but there were some lengthy word problems so time was an issue Were aptitude questions same for everyone?+1+1+1+1

IIT Dhanbad 

Role: C++ Developer

Platform : Hackerrank

Webcam: No

Duration :2 hrs

Total 27 questions: 20 mcqs comprising  OS, DBMS and CN +1 sql  query + 6 coding questions.

Questions were of moderate level like finding connected components of a graph, finding 4th bit of a number when represented in binary.

1 dp question of gfg, 2 implementations and finding max path sum in a tree.

Role: Full Stack Developer

Platform: Hackerrank

Webcam: No

Duration: 2hrs

15 MCQ(multiple options correct) : Topics include - Web development(Referential integrity, TCP, UDP, REST, JWT, Session, JSON, Caching etc), DSA(Sorting, array, linkedlist etc)

2 Coding - Easy-medium level

1 API based coding - (in Javascript/Python/Ruby/Go/C# etc)

IIT BHU

  1. Find number subsequences in a given array that only have unique continuous numbers. solution anyone? Store the frequency in a frq[] array and then dp relation would be dp[i]=frq[i]+dp[i-1]*frq[i]. That’s it.
  2.  You have some old pencils and some dollars, you can sell each pencil for a ‘x’ extra dollars, or you can give a pencil and ‘y’ dollars to get a new pencil. How many maximum new pencils can you have? Binary Search solution solves all TCs. // A general formula was being generated if you looked closely. Make sure to typecast each input to long long before using. Formula was ((dollars+(pencils*x))/(x+y)). This will give the max new pencils.

           Soln to both ques pls??fa

IIT BHU (Same for IITR)

2 Questions, 3 hours, Hacker earth

  1. Given a string s, and a grid or chars, Find the max number of times you can get s repeating, with starting point (0,0) you can traverse in all 8 directions. (grid size is 15x15 max)

  1. Given N boxes and integer k, each having 5$ coins ando 10$ coins, the probability of getting a 5$ coin in each box is given in an array ‘arr’. Select 2*k boxes such that by taking a coin from each the probability of getting k 5$ coins and k 10$ coins is max.

Please Add Ques here!!!+1+1

30 MCQs based on Algo,OS, Data structures, Sql, dbms, ML and 4 coding questions in 2 hr 30 min.

MCQs- What are types of binary Tree (complete tree, proper tree, etc), Quicksort, MergeSort, Time Complexity of different sorting algo, Basics of neural networks, SELECT/ ALTER statements in SQL, etc. Basic knowledge of these topics are sufficient to solve MCQs!!

  1. Given a sorted array of numbers, return repeated numbers in array if no repeated found return {-1}.Did anyone pass last two test cases?No++++/ I think it can be done using unordered map to store count of keys and then getting the keys in the final array and sorting that final array.Not Worked!!++ THEN WHAT WILL WORKNothing will work

Input: {1,2,2,3,4,4,5} output: {2,4}

input: {1,7,8,9}        output: {-1}

  1. Given two integers m and n, assume matrix of dimensions of m*n and you are initially at (1,1). You have to reach (m,n) in minimum steps with condition next step can be (i+j,j) or (i,i+j) from (i,j).  

Input: 2 3  Output:2 (solution: if gcd of src and des is not 1 then return -1 else move from destination to source)(Dp se 6 paas huye ?YES)(normal maths + greedy sae hojayega) (without dp wala sol dedo koi)Can be solved using recursion just go from (n, m) to (1, 1) not (1, 1) to (n, m)this passed all tcs for me)

  1. Evaluate Postfix Expressionuse stack
  2. 0-1 KnapSack The sample test case 2 will have an error in the question statement. It is correct for the compiler

Tip: If you are a c++ user then make sure to select c++17 from the language list. The language writte

n as cpp is c++11 and c++17 is lower in the list.

IIT Dhanbad

  • Given an array A with N integers, reorder the elements of A such that after a particular reordering of elements, let the array be B, Then let

Fi  = bitwise & of prefix till i. Find the minimum possible sum(Fi) for all i in B.  soln??++

  • You are given an undirected weighted graph that contains N+1 nodes and M edges. These nodes are numbered from 0 to N. Initially, you start at

node 0 Each node (except node 0) has a priority associated with it that is denoted by the array denoted as P. You have to follow these commands

to visit each node in the path:

  1. You have to start at node 0 and move to the next unvisited node which is directly connected to node 0 and having the highest priority
  2. If the priority is the same for multiple nodes, then you have to select the nodes that have the minimum distance between them.
  3. After going to the next node, you have to again select a connected node that has the highest priority among the remaining unvisited nodes
  4. If there are no adjacent unvisited nodes at a point, then you have to traverse back to the previous node from where you came to the present node for the first time.

Find the time of visit for each node if we follow this algo.        

  • Count all numbers from the range L to R (inclusive) that have exactly one zero in its binary representation.(Constraints) not sure but constraints were large may be 10^3  
  • You are given the following
  • N :  An integer
  • A : An array of integers of length N
  • B : An array of integers of length N
  • You can perform the following operation any number of times: Pick any two elements of array B and swap them.
  • Task : You are required to find the minimum value of sum( | Ai - Bi | ).

different people got different questions others had more easier questions

Q. Given n points (x and y coordinates) find the minimum number of lines needed to join them n<=100000.

Q. Number of occurrences of a word in string? Case sensitivity was ignored.

  1. Brute force solution was accepted.

https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/frequency-substring-string/ 

how to solve the 2nd q??????

As the question was not clear, I assumed the chairs in the round table are fixed i.e, ‘N’. Given below is the approach for this case.Example of a circular component : 1->2, 2->3,3->4,4->1 .

https://leetcode.com/playground/iBM5TMbb

For the other case(some chairs can be abandoned so that we can invite one circular component), It’s the maximum of the answer you get in this approach and the maximum length among the circular components in the graph.

IITG

IIT BHU (There were different sets) platform ?? Hackerrank

        Coding -

  1. Coin change problem with number of combinations (not number of permutations) DP
  2. Subset that give maximum sum <= target  Backtracking
  3. Given 3 groups which have n1(groupA) , n2(groupB) and n3 (groupC)elements. The elements of each group are distinct and lie between 1 and n . We perform some operations on the groups , and after performing these operations each of these groups are sorted and appended in the following order groupA , groupB,groupC . An operation is such that one element s moved from one group to another. How many minimum operation need to be done such that after doing these operations and sorting the 3 groups independently and then appending them in the specified order , we will get a strictly increasing sequence

Answer - create a vector v = gA + gB + gC . The answer is v.size() - LIS(v) NOTE: LIS must be found in O(nlogn)

IITH (there were different questions for different people):

  1. Lifting Weights Roblox OA | New Grad 2021 - LeetCode Discuss 
  2. Secret Array Amazon | Online Assessment July 2021 | Secret Array - LeetCode Discuss
  3. [HARD] https://imgur.com/a/0BWoTRm

Schlumberger

IITD (on 25/10)

  • 3 sections, 90 min, proctored - camera on, screen recording on, any coding language for section 3 but only java for 1 and 2.
  • 1st section - mcq based on java, 20 questions, 10minutes, no negative marking.
  • 2nd section - mcq based on java code snippet, 10 questions, 10minutes, no negative marking.
  • 3rd section - coding, 2 questions, 60 min, very easy questions, a CS student can solve both questions in less than 20 min.
  • 1st question - check if a number lies in fibonacci series or not (n numbers will be given)
  • 2nd question - return binary of decimal (n integers will be given)

Cred

IITG(27/10) Data scientist profile

2 coding 12 mcq

  1. The computing cluster has multiple processors, each with 4 cores. The number of tasks to handle is equal to the total number of cores in the cluster. Each task has a predicted execution time and each processor has a specified time when its core becomes available. Assuming that exactly 4 tasks are assigned to each processor and those tasks run independently(asynchronously) on the cores of the chosen processor, what is the earliest time that all tasks can be processed.

https://cs.stackexchange.com/questions/143535/hackerrank-august-2021-coding-challenge-minimum-time-needed-to-finish-all-task

  1. Question statement was planting minimum bombs. But we have to do exactly this: https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/count-minimum-number-of-fountains-to-be-activated-to-cover-the-entire-garden/

Rupeek(SDE)(WAS THERE ANY CG SHORTLIST FOR TEST??)NO

IIT BHU

MCQ - DBMS , SQL , C Language , OOPS

Coding

1)There are N tasks . Task i can be done at position task[i][0] and requires task[i][1] time to be done . It takes 1 unit time to cover 1 unit distance . Initially we are at position 0 determine the maximum number of tasks we can do and return back to 0 within time T

 eg - task = { {2,8} {4,5} ,{5,1}}  T = 16

 ans = 2

2)2 players playing a game . In each turn a player can remove a card from end or beginning of the deck .Player 1 goes first. Both players play optimally i.e with the intention to maximize their own score . Return the min absolute difference between the score of the 2 players .

https://leetcode.com/problems/stone-game/

3)Count number of subarrays , whose length = k times the sum of the elements of the subarray

Ans - multiply each element of the array by K , and then subtract 1 from them . Now the problem reduces to finding number of subarrays with sum = 0; This works because k*(A[i] + A[i+1] + .. A[j] ) = (j-i+1) => k*A[i]-1 + k*A[i+1] -1 + k*A[i+2] - 1 + ....... + k*A[j] - 1 = 0

OR

1)Given n, find number of ordered pair value of (x,y,z) where (x*y)+z=n. n<=1e5 and x,y,z should be greater than 0.

Example:n=3 output:3 (1,1,2),(1,2,1),(2,1,1)

2)The problem reduced to finding nCk in O(1) and there were n queries with each query having k in {1,n}.

Precompute all the factorials in an array , long long fact[n+1]

3)Implement a binary search tree , create a TreeNode struct and add nodes to the BST.Upen adding nodes obtain the left view of the tree , and check if this forms a palindrome or not

        Constraints

Given a weighted undirected graph and a source node s such that all the nodes are connected. We travel from the source node to each city such that total path length is minimum. Now for each node, find the number of possible predecessors in all optimal paths from source to that node (predecessors are all the nodes in the path except source node and that node itself).

Given n < 104

 Ex.

number of nodes, n = 4

number of edges, m = 4

source node s = 1

E1-> 1-2, weight w = 1

E2-> 2-3, weight w = 1

E3-> 1-4, weight w = 1

E4-> 4-3, weight w = 1

ans = [0, 0, 2, 0]

for node 1-> zero length path.

for node 2-> path with length 1, 1->2. no predecessor.

for node 3-> two possible shortest paths. 1->2->3 and 1->4->3, total possible predecessors = 1+1 = 2

for node 4-> path is 1->4. no predecessor.

        

        Q3. https://cs.stackexchange.com/questions/144454/print-all-nodes-which-are-the-endpoint-of-the-diameter-of-a-tree

 IITBHU                     Solutions bata do guys please(Is ss allowed ?)

In 1st ques create priority queue with {stones of node, node}. Now select node having most no. of stones and change its adjacent nodes’ stones to required minimum if they are not balanced and put them again in queue, if they are balanced already then dont push them in queue. loop until queue is empty. keep track of stones changed.

1.

        In 2nd ques create vector of intervals and sort them. now make an array say dp[n] and from end of array keep track of no. of sets upto that interval from the end. Now iterate over every element(ai,bi) from i=0 and select the rightmost interval(aj,bj) where aj-bi<=k, now check no. of sets remaining if (ai,bi) and (aj,bj) were connected with some (bi,aj) interval with the help of dp[n]. Check for the min of such sets remaining. Selection of rightmost interval can be done with binary search.

3.

 IIT BHU

  1. Number of distinct segments. Given some intervals. You have to find the minimum number of intervals possible after merging. if you are allowed to add one interval of maximum k size. (Based on merge overlapping intervals).

        -> first merge all the intervals then all the intervals present are non overlapping. Now find the minimum interval possible after adding one interval of k size.888

IIT BHU

  1. Given n seats. Adjacent seats can’t be occupied i.e if a person sits at seat i-1 and i+1 should be empty.

given an n size array having elements either 0 or 1. (0 empty and 1 filled).

Task:Give answer true or false whether it is possible to occupy k more people in that row.

  1. A string is called pseudo palindrome if any of its permutations is palindrome. Given a string you have to answer the number of distinct pseudo palindrome substring possible in that string. eg.  AAC is pseudo palindrome and ACD is not pseudo palindrome.
  2. Given an array two players are playing the game. Player 1 starts the game. Player 1 can take elements either from start or from end. Player 2 can take elements only from the start. Find the maximum possible score Player 1 can get.

IITR (Sprinklr)

  1. Color tree-   Link? Please
  2. String encoding - to reduce the length of string by replacing repeated string with k where k is no. of times the string is repeated.

ex: given --- faabcaabcde , length = 11

ans = f2[aabc]de length = 10

return 10;

  1. Given a array {1,5,9} replace the digit with sum of subarray(at least size of two) b/w i &j till single digit remains at cost (j-i+1)*min(subarr(i,j))’ Return max cost.

(i=0,j=1)--arr = {6,9} ,cost = (1-0+1)*1= 2   ----> (i=0,j=1)arr = {15} ,cost = (2)*6=12 Total cost = 2+12= 14

(i=0, j=2)arr = {15}, cost = (2-0+1)*1= 3; total cost= 3;

return max(14,3)= 14;

HOW TO OPTIMIZE Ques 6(BTADO PLEASE)+++++

Q7 SOLN OR LOGIC(+++++)

AbinBev

Coding Que-

1) Reducing Dishes

Suppose there is a chef. And he has collected data on the satisfaction level of his n dishes. The Chef can cook any dish in 1 unit of time. Like-time coefficient of a dish is actually the time taken

to cook that dish including previous dishes multiplied by its satisfaction level So time[i]*satisfaction[i].

We have to find the maximum sum of Like-time coefficient that the chef can obtain after dishes preparation. Dishes can be ready in any order and the chef can discard some dishes to get this maximum value.

So, if the input is like [-1,-7,0,6,-7], then the output will be 17, After removing second and last dish, the maximum total like-time coefficient will be -1*1 + 0*2 + 6*3 = 17.
